attacking_style: Sprint finisher who excels in final 200m with explosive acceleration, particularly effective on uphill finishes and selective parcours. Rarely attacks early; relies on positioning and superior closing speed.
team_tactics: Team execution inconsistent—multiple failures to deliver despite having fastest finisher. When functioning, they position him well but chase strategy has proven problematic. Rider often salvages results through individual quality.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finishes and brutal selective conditions (wet Paris-Roubaix, hilly stages) where pure sprinters are dropped. Thrives when attrition removes fast-twitch specialists but finish remains bunch sprint.
vulnerability: Dependent on team execution which has failed repeatedly (at least 2 lost opportunities at Dauphiné alone). When team pacing/chase strategy fails, even superior speed cannot salvage the win.
key_patterns: Superior final 200m acceleration repeatedly underestimated, catches riders from seemingly impossible distances (2 instances)
Rare sprinter profile: survives selective climbs and tough conditions while retaining sprint dominance (3 races)
Team execution failures cost wins despite being fastest finisher (3 documented failures)
Excels when opponents jump early; benefits from positioning behind premature attacks before unleashing finish
Performs best in attritional conditions that eliminate pure sprinters but allow powerful finishers to survive
form_trajectory: positive: Peaked brilliantly through mid-2021, culminating in Paris-Roubaix Monument victory in October after demonstrating consistent sprint superiority and rare ability to survive brutal conditions throughout season
